title: Forced Exit Burst Vs Initiative Continuation
focus_questions: Was the move mainly trapped-side exits, or did the new side prove durable control after the burst?
After the release slowed down, was gained ground actually defended?

summary.thesis: A fast squeeze or flush is often only forced exits until the new side proves it can still hold and re-accelerate after the release fuel fades.
summary.operator_translation: Do not call a vertical burst fresh initiative unless the gaining side defends the new ground after trapped exits are mostly done.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when a move is fast, emotional, and tied to inventory pain, and the replay needs to separate release fuel from true control transfer.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: a burst follows failed defense, failed reclaim, trap release, or inventory unwind. Required evidence: clear release source, post-burst hold quality, and proof that the new side either defends or fails after the exits fade. Do not trade: by chasing the vertical burst before new control is proven. Real trigger: first shallow pullback holds and the gaining side re-accelerates, or failure after the burst confirms it was exit fuel only. Failure point: the gained ground cannot hold once the trapped exits are spent.

when_relevant: A vertical burst follows a failed level, failed reclaim, trap release, or harvest completion.
The move looks strong enough to invite continuation chasing.
The operator needs to know whether the burst is mostly release fuel or a true takeover.
required_evidence: there is a plausible source of trapped or forced exits
the burst can be separated from the first meaningful pullback afterward
the gaining side either defends the new ground or quickly loses it once the burst slows
confirmation_signals: the burst begins after a clear trap, failure, or forced unwind becomes active
the first pullback after the burst shows whether new control exists
continuation only becomes credible if the gaining side can hold and re-accelerate after the exit fuel fades
invalidation_signals: there is no clear release source and the burst is being over-labeled
the gained ground fails immediately once the burst slows
the new side never proves defense after the trapped exits are mostly done
no_trade_conditions: Do not chase a vertical burst before testing whether it is mostly forced exit fuel.
Do not assume new initiative exists if the gained ground is not defended on the first meaningful retest.
Do not fade the burst only because it is vertical if the new side is actually holding and rebuilding.

entry_archetypes.name: post-burst hold and re-acceleration
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the first pullback holds and the gaining side proves the move is more than trapped-side exit fuel.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the pullback already widens into repair or balance.

entry_archetypes.name: exit-fuel failure reversion
entry_archetypes.description: Enter when the burst loses its gained ground and the market proves the move was mostly forced exits rather than new control.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the gaining side is still defending the post-burst area.

management_notes: The best continuation read usually comes after the first shallow pullback holds.
If post-burst balance widens quickly, the move was often more exit fuel than true control transfer.
Once the exit fuel is spent, shift target logic from emotional release to accepted auction structure.
review_questions: Was the operator chasing trapped-side exits and calling them fresh initiative?
Did the new side defend gained ground after the burst, or only enjoy the release?
When the burst slowed down, did the market continue with control or slip into repair and balance?
